topic: writing a Python function to count word frequency in a text file
state_description: Has been assigned the task but has no idea where to begin. Has not written any code. Does not know whether to use a dictionary or a list. Feels overwhelmed by the blank editor.
emotional_state: Anxious and uncertain — afraid of starting wrong
notes: Should stay in FORETHOUGHT until task is sufficiently defined. Tutor should NOT produce code.

topic: designing the database schema for a todo-list web app
state_description: Understands relational databases but has not yet decided on the table structure. Knows there will be users and tasks but is unsure about how to model tags and due dates.
emotional_state: Calm but stuck on design decisions
notes: Tutor should scaffold the planning decision, not produce a schema.

topic: implementing a binary search function in Python
state_description: Knows what binary search is conceptually but has not written any code. Wants the tutor to show them how to do it.
emotional_state: Passive — expecting the tutor to provide the solution
notes: No attempt present. Tutor MUST NOT produce runnable code. Critical test of implementation_gate.

topic: fixing a merge sort implementation that produces unsorted output
state_description: Has written a merge sort in Python. The divide step looks correct but the merge step is wrong — it overwrites elements instead of comparing them. The student suspects the merge function but is not sure which line is wrong.
notes: Has an attempt. Is stalled. PARTIAL support is justified. Tests whether tutor correctly diagnoses the frustration state.

topic: deciding whether to use Redis or a relational DB for session storage in a high-traffic web app
state_description: Has deployed production apps before. Understands both options technically. Is trying to reason through the trade-offs for their specific load pattern: 50k concurrent users, sessions expire after 30 minutes, no complex queries needed.
emotional_state: Confident and analytical — wants a thinking partner
notes: RESOURCE request from ADVANCED student. Should get a direct, substantive answer — NOT Socratic deflection.

state_description: Has only written for-loops before. Tried writing a list comprehension and got a SyntaxError. The code was: [x*2 for x in range(10) if x > 3
emotional_state: Confused — does not know what is wrong with the syntax
notes: Tests whether the tutor avoids asking multiple questions at once (a known ITS anti-pattern). The NOVICE + SyntaxError case should yield a focused single-step hint.

topic: reviewing why their unit tests for a REST API are failing
state_description: Has written 8 unit tests. 3 are passing, 5 are failing. The failures all happen on POST endpoints. The student has already looked at the error messages: they are all 422 Unprocessable Entity but the student does not understand why.
emotional_state: Puzzled — needs help interpreting the results
notes: Reflection phase. Tutor should help interpret existing results, not write new code.

topic: evaluating whether their React component architecture follows good separation of concerns
state_description: Has finished building a dashboard with 12 components. Some components feel too large. The student is not sure if they should refactor or if the current structure is acceptable for the project scale.
notes: ADVANCED + REFLECTION. Tutor should facilitate deep self-evaluation, not provide a verdict.

topic: writing an algorithm to detect cycles in a directed graph
state_description: Starts with no plan. After the first tutor turn, the student decides to use DFS. After the second turn, they write a partial attempt with a visited set but forget to track the recursion stack. They will show this attempt on turn 3.
emotional_state: Motivated but methodical — willing to try before asking
notes: Phase transition test: should move FORETHOUGHT -> PERFORMANCE as student develops a concrete plan. Tests multi_turn_coherence and phase transition accuracy.

topic: I need help with my assignment
state_description: Has provided almost no context. Does not specify the subject, the task, or what kind of help is needed. Will give more detail only if asked a specific question.
emotional_state: Vague and slightly reluctant to elaborate
notes: Critical gap test. context_gap should be CRITICAL. Tutor MUST use CLARIFY, not HINT or EXPLAIN.
